explanation: Only 2 of 5 concepts (one-way functions via the East Australian Current, avalanche effect via Nemo touching the boat) have even partial mechanism-level matches, while collision resistance, salting, and Merkle trees have no analogous mechanism anywhere in the film.
alternatives: Memento (2000) — irreversible memory loss maps to one-wayness, and Leonard's tattoos behave like tamper-evident commitments
The Imitation Game (2014) — a genuinely cryptographic setting with adversaries, giving natural homes to collision hunting and salting-style countermeasures
Sneakers (1992) — an adversarial cryptography plot where verification, integrity, and 'no way back once broken' mechanics appear on screen
